pi_items.source: setup_task.sh:18 creates input with 'node=workshop&json={workshop_power:150}'; export_result.sh:14 confirms INPUT_NAME='workshop_power'
export_result.sh:29 explicitly states '40 - 45 = -5'; verifier.py:61 validates Test A expects 0 for input 40 (implying 45W parasitic load)
export_result.sh:13 defines FEED_NAME='workshop_production_load'; setup_task.sh:33 confirms same feed name to be created
export_result.sh:31 sets TEST_A_EXPECT=0; verifier.py:61-68 validates this as clamping logic (40 - 45 = -5 → 0)
export_result.sh:49 sets TEST_B_EXPECT=100; verifier.py:72 validates subtraction logic (145 - 45 = 100)

privileged_info_summary: The configure_parasitic_load_filter task requires configuring Emoncms input processing to subtract 45W from workshop_power input and clamp negative results to 0, logging to workshop_production_load feed at 10s interval. All metadata claims are verified: input_name is workshop_power; parasitic_load is 45W; target_feed_name is workshop_production_load; test_case_low_input is 40 (expects 0 output after 40-45=-5 clamped); test_case_high_input is 145 (expects 100 output after 145-45=100). Emoncms supports required operations: offset processor for subtraction, allow_positive for clamping, and PHPFina feeds support 10-second intervals. Data is synthetic, created by setup script posting test values.
